};

font-weight: 500; line-height: 26px; letter-spacing: 0px;">typescript</div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; overflow-x: auto;"><pre><code><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">type</span><span> Page = </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"home"</span><span> | </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"about"</span><span> | </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"contact"</span><span>;

</span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">interface</span><span> PageInfo {
  title: </span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">string</span><span>;
}

</span><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;">// Every key in 'Page' MUST be present in this object</span><span>
</span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">const</span><span> nav: Record&lt;Page, PageInfo&gt; = {
  home: { title: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"Welcome Home"</span><span> },
  about: { title: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"About Us"</span><span> },
  contact: { title: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"Get in Touch"</span><span> }
};
</span></code></pre></div></div><div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px 4px 16px 16px; margin-top: 3px; overflow: hidden; padding: 12px 16px;"><div style="font-size: 12px;">Use code with caution.</div><button style="margin: 0px; background: none; color: inherit; border: none; font: inherit; outline: inherit; cursor: pointer; height: 44px; width: 44px; display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; border-radius: 50%;"></button></div></div></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: var(--uBdgTe); font-weight: var(--Zf9Xnd); line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">3. font-weight: 500; line-height: 26px; letter-spacing: 0px;">typescript</div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; overflow-x: auto;"><pre><code><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">enum</span><span> TaskStatus {
  Todo = </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"TODO"</span><span>,
  InProgress = </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"IN_PROGRESS"</span><span>,
  Done = </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"DONE"</span><span>
}

</span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">const</span><span> statusColors: Record&lt;TaskStatus, </span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">string</span><span>&gt; = {
  [TaskStatus.Todo]: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"red"</span><span>,
  [TaskStatus.InProgress]: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"orange"</span><span>,
  [TaskStatus.Done]: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"green"</span><span>
};
</span></code></pre></div></div><div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px 4px 16px 16px; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Type</code><span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">optional</strong>. Useful for update payloads where you might only change a few fields.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Required&lt;Type&gt;</code></strong>: The opposite of<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Partial</code>; it makes all optional properties<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">mandatory</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Readonly&lt;Type&gt;</code></strong>: Marks all properties as<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">read-only</strong>, preventing them from being reassigned after initialization.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Record&lt;Keys, Type&gt;</code></strong>: Constructs an object type with specific<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Keys</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(usually a union) and a uniform<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Type</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>for all values.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Pick&lt;Type, Keys&gt;</code></strong>: Creates a new type by<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">selecting</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>a specific set of properties from an existing one.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Omit&lt;Type, Keys&gt;</code></strong>: The opposite of<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Pick</code>; it creates a type by<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">removing</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>specific properties from an existing one.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Exclude&lt;UnionType, ExcludedMembers&gt;</code></strong>: Removes specific members from a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">union type</strong>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Extract&lt;Type, Union&gt;</code></strong>: Keeps only the members of a union that are<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">assignable</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>to another specified type.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">null</code><span>&nbsp;</span>and<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">undefined</code><span>&nbsp;</span>from a type.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Record</code>, TypeScript will throw a compile-time error if you forget to define a value for even one of the enum members. This acts as a safety net when your code grows.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ol style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 20px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Guaranteed Coverage</strong>: If you add a new member to your<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">enum</code><span>&nbsp;</span>later (e.g., adding<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Archived</code><span>&nbsp;</span>to a<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Status</code><span>&nbsp;</span>enum), TypeScript will immediately red-line every<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Record</code><span>&nbsp;</span>using that enum until you provide a value for the new status.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">No "Magic Strings"</strong>: It prevents typos because you are referencing<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Status.Done</code><span>&nbsp;</span>instead of typing<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">"done"</code><span>&nbsp;</span>manually in multiple places.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Cleaner than Switch/Case</strong>: Instead of a bulky<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">switch</code><span>&nbsp;</span>statement to find a color or label for a status, you can use a simple, type-safe lookup:<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">StatusColors[status]</code>.</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 20px; align-items: center; box-sizing: border-box; overflow: hidden; position: relative; max-inline-size: 250px; vertical-align: text-top; height: 20px; padding: 0px 0px 0px 3px; width: 20px; block-size: 20px; inline-size: auto; padding-block: 3px; padding-inline: 6px; border-radius: 20px; height: 12px; margin-right: 4px; width: 12px; flex-shrink: 0;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--IXoxUe); font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; display: flex; overflow: hidden; min-width: 0px; font-weight: 500;"><span style="overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; min-width: 0px;">Stack Overflow</span><span style="flex-shrink: 0;">&nbsp;+5</span></span></div></button></span></span></li></ol><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Comparison: Enum vs. Union<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">While you can achieve similar exhaustiveness with<span>&nbsp;</span><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;">Union Types</span><span>&nbsp;</span>(e.g.,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">'todo' | 'done'</code>), enums are often preferred for:<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; overflow-x: auto;"><pre><code><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">enum</span><span> UserRole {
  Admin = </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"ADMIN"</span><span>,
  User = </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"USER"</span><span>,
  Guest = </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"GUEST"</span><span>
}

</span><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;">// Error: Property 'GUEST' is missing in type...</span><span>
</span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">const</span><span> RoleLabels: Record&lt;UserRole, </span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">string</span><span>&gt; = {
  [UserRole.Admin]: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"Administrator"</span><span>,
  [UserRole.User]: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"Regular User"</span><span>,
  </span><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;">// If you forget Guest, TS flags it here!</span><span>
};
</span></code></pre></div></div><div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px 4px 16px 16px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">Choosing between an<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Enum</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>and a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Union</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(<code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">'Admin' | 'User'</code>)<span>&nbsp;</span><mark style="color: var(--jINu6c); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--jwFC2e); padding: 0px 2px; font-weight: 500;">usually comes down to whether you want a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">data structure</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>or just a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">type</strong></mark>.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">When to use Enums<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Values at Runtime:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Enums exist as actual objects in your JavaScript code. This makes them great for iterating (e.g.,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Object.values(UserRole)</code>) to build dropdown menus or lists.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Mapping/Refactoring:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>If you change<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Admin = "ADMIN"</code><span>&nbsp;</span>to<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Admin = "SUPER_ADMIN"</code><span>&nbsp;</span>in the enum definition, it updates everywhere in your app automatically.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Metadata:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>You can attach JSDoc comments to enum members, which show up in your IDE's autocomplete.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">When to use Unions (The Modern TS Preference)<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Lightweight:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Unions disappear completely after compilation. There is zero overhead in your final JavaScript bundle.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Better Compatibility:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Unions work seamlessly with external JSON or API data. You don't have to "cast" a string from an API to an enum; if the string matches the union, it just works.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Readability:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">type Status = 'open' | 'closed'</code><span>&nbsp;</span>is often easier to read and write than defining a separate<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">enum</code><span>&nbsp;</span>block.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; Reach for<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Enums</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>only if you need to loop through the values at runtime or want a centralized place to manage values that might change in the future.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; flex-direction: row;"><span role="heading" style="-webkit-box-orient: vertical; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; display: -webkit-box; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; overflow-wrap: break-word; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--m3c11,#eef0ff); position: relative; font-weight: 400; --m3c9: #e6e8f0; --m3c11: #e6e8f0; display: flex;"><div style="width: 652.438px;"><div><div style="display: contents;"></div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; width: 652px; max-width: var(--CYi3c--fRcLaf,652px);"><div style="display: contents;"><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);"><div style="display: contents;">To get the benefits of a<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Union</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(lightweight) with the benefits of an<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Enum</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(iteratable), you use a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">const</code><span>&nbsp;</span>assertion</strong>.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">This is the modern TypeScript way to handle a list of options.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">The "Const Array" Pattern<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div style="margin-block: 24px 32px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: var(--jINu6c); font-weight: 400;"><div><div style="background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 16px 16px 4px 4px; overflow: auto hidden; padding: 12px 16px 0px;"><div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between;"><div style="font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 26px; letter-spacing: 0px;">typescript</div></div><div dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; overflow-x: auto;"><pre><code><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;">// 1. Create a constant array (must use 'as const')</span><span>
</span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">const</span><span> COLORS = [</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"red"</span><span>, </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"green"</span><span>, </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"blue"</span><span>] </span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">as</span><span> </span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">const</span><span>;

</span><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;">// 2. Derive the Union type automatically from the array</span><span>
</span><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;">// This creates: type Color = "red" | "green" | "blue"</span><span>
</span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">type</span><span> Color = </span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">typeof</span><span> COLORS[</span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">number</span><span>];

</span><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;">// 3. Use it in a Record for exhaustiveness</span><span>
</span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">const</span><span> ColorHex: Record&lt;Color, </span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">string</span><span>&gt; = {
  red: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"#FF0000"</span><span>,
  green: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"#00FF00"</span><span>,
  blue: </span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"#0000FF"</span><span>
};

</span><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;">// 4. Since it's a real array, you can loop over it for UI (like a dropdown)</span><span>
COLORS.forEach(color =&gt; {
  console.log(</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">`The hex for ${</span><span>color</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">} is ${</span><span>ColorHex[color]</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">}`</span><span>);
});
</span></code></pre></div></div><div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px 4px 16px 16px; margin-top: 3px; overflow: hidden; padding: 12px 16px;"><div style="font-size: 12px;">Use code with caution.</div><button style="margin: 0px; background: none; color: inherit; border: none; font: inherit; outline: inherit; cursor: pointer; height: 44px; width: 44px; display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; border-radius: 50%;"></button></div></div></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">Why this beats an Enum:<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ol style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 20px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Zero Mapping:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>If you get<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">"red"</code><span>&nbsp;</span>from an API, it automatically matches the<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">Color</code><span>&nbsp;</span>type. You don't have to do<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">ColorEnum["red"]</code>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Single Source of Truth:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>You define the values once in an array. The type and the runtime list are perfectly synced.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">No Boilerplate:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>You don't have to write a separate<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">enum</code><span>&nbsp;</span>block and then a separate<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">type</code>.</span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ol><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; Here is the breakdown of why those two weird pieces are necessary:</div><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">1. The<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">as const</code><span>&nbsp;</span>(The "Lock")<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">By default, TypeScript sees<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">["red", "green"]</code><span>&nbsp;</span>and thinks:<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">"This is an array of any strings."</em><br>When you add<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">as const</code>, you are telling TS:<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">"This array will<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">never</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>change, and these specific strings are the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">only</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>values allowed."</em><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Without<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">as const</code></strong>:<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">type</code><span>&nbsp;</span>=<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">string[]</code></span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">With<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">as const</code></strong>:<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">type</code><span>&nbsp;</span>=<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">readonly ["red", "green"]</code></span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">2. The<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">typeof COLORS[number]</code><span>&nbsp;</span>(The "Extractor")<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">This is a trick to turn an<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Array</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(runtime) into a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Union Type</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(compile-time).<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">typeof COLORS</code></strong>: Gets the type of the whole array.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;"><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">[number]</code></strong>: This tells TypeScript:<span>&nbsp;</span><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: italic;">"Look at the type of<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">any</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>element at a numeric index inside that array."</em></span><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></li></ul><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">Since the array is "locked" with<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">as const</code>, the only things at those indexes are<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">"red"</code>,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">"green"</code>, and<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">"blue"</code>. So, the resulting type is<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">"red" | "green" | "blue"</code>.<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">The "Why"<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><div style="font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word; margin-block: var(--xdqYEb--pNrv5d,12px) var(--xdqYEb--i8AQde,16px);">It feels weird because you are bridging two worlds:<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ol style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 20px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: decimal;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">The Runtime World:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>You need the<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">COLORS</code><span>&nbsp;</span>array so you can use<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; # 1

typescript record meaning example

---

In TypeScript, the Record<Keys, Type> is a utility type used to define an object's structure by specifying exactly what its keys and values should be. [1, 2] 
It is essentially a shorthand for creating a "dictionary" or "map" where all values share the same type. [3, 4] 
Basic Syntax

```ts
Record<K, T>
```


* K (Keys): The type of the property keys (must be string, number, or symbol).
* T (Type): The type of the values associated with those keys. [5, 6, 7, 8] 

Common Examples1. Simple Dictionary
If you want an object where any string can be a key and every value must be a number:

```ts
const scores: Record<string, number> = {
  "Alice": 10,
  "Bob": 15
};
```

2. Restricting Keys (Union Types)
You can use Record to ensure an object contains a specific set of keys. This is one of its most powerful uses. [1, 9, 10] 

```ts
type Page = "home" | "about" | "contact";
interface PageInfo {
  title: string;
}
// Every key in 'Page' MUST be present in this object
const nav: Record<Page, PageInfo> = {
  home: { title: "Welcome Home" },
  about: { title: "About Us" },
  contact: { title: "Get in Touch" }
};
```

3. Using Enums as Keys
Record is often used with enums to ensure every possible case is handled. [2, 10] 

```ts
enum TaskStatus {
  Todo = "TODO",
  InProgress = "IN_PROGRESS",
  Done = "DONE"
}
const statusColors: Record<TaskStatus, string> = {
  [TaskStatus.Todo]: "red",
  [TaskStatus.InProgress]: "orange",
  [TaskStatus.Done]: "green"
};
```

Why use Record over a standard interface?

* Brevity: It is much faster to write Record<string, number> than defining a full index signature like { [key: string]: number }.
* Exhaustiveness: When used with a union (like in the "Page" example above), TypeScript will throw an error if you forget to include one of the keys or add an extra one.
* Mapping: It is ideal for "transforming" one type into another (e.g., taking a list of IDs and mapping them to User objects). [6, 9, 10, 11, 12] 

Would you like to see how to combine Record with other utility types like Partial or Readonly?

# 2

what are the util types in ts

---

[TypeScript](https://www.typescriptlang.org/) provides several built-in utility types that allow you to transform existing types into new ones without rewriting them. They are globally available and use generics under the hood to handle common type manipulations. [1, 2, 3] 
Common Object Transformation Types

* Partial<Type>: Makes all properties in Type optional. Useful for update payloads where you might only change a few fields.
* Required<Type>: The opposite of Partial; it makes all optional properties mandatory.
* Readonly<Type>: Marks all properties as read-only, preventing them from being reassigned after initialization.
* Record<Keys, Type>: Constructs an object type with specific Keys (usually a union) and a uniform Type for all values.
* Pick<Type, Keys>: Creates a new type by selecting a specific set of properties from an existing one.
* Omit<Type, Keys>: The opposite of Pick; it creates a type by removing specific properties from an existing one. [1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8] 

Union and Nullable Types

* Exclude<UnionType, ExcludedMembers>: Removes specific members from a union type.
* Extract<Type, Union>: Keeps only the members of a union that are assignable to another specified type.
* NonNullable<Type>: Removes null and undefined from a type. [1, 4, 8] 

Function and Async Types

* ReturnType<Type>: Extracts the return type of a function.
* Parameters<Type>: Extracts the types of a function's parameters as a tuple.
* Awaited<Type>: Models the unwrapping of a Promise, similar to using await or .then(). [1, 4, 8, 9, 10, 11] 

String Manipulation Types
These types are used to transform string literal types: [11] 

* Uppercase<S>, Lowercase<S>: Converts string literal types to all upper or lower case.
* Capitalize<S>, Uncapitalize<S>: Capitalizes (or uncapitalizes) the first letter of a string literal type. [12, 13] 

For official definitions and advanced examples, refer to the [TypeScript Documentation on Utility Types](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/utility-types.html). [9] 
Do you want a deep dive into conditional types or how to create your own custom utility types?

# 3

why enum example

---

Combining Record with an enum is a best practice because it forces exhaustiveness. [1, 2, 3] 
If you use an enum as the key for a Record, TypeScript will throw a compile-time error if you forget to define a value for even one of the enum members. This acts as a safety net when your code grows. [2, 3] 
Why this specific pattern is powerful:

   1. Guaranteed Coverage: If you add a new member to your enum later (e.g., adding Archived to a Status enum), TypeScript will immediately red-line every Record using that enum until you provide a value for the new status.
   2. No "Magic Strings": It prevents typos because you are referencing Status.Done instead of typing "done" manually in multiple places.
   3. Cleaner than Switch/Case: Instead of a bulky switch statement to find a color or label for a status, you can use a simple, type-safe lookup: StatusColors[status]. [2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7] 

Comparison: Enum vs. Union
While you can achieve similar exhaustiveness with Union Types (e.g., 'todo' | 'done'), enums are often preferred for: [8] 

* Documentation: You can add JSDoc comments to each enum member that appear in your IDE.
* Refactoring: Changing an enum value in one place updates it everywhere it is referenced. [9, 10] 

Example of the "Exhaustiveness" Error

enum UserRole {
  Admin = "ADMIN",
  User = "USER",
  Guest = "GUEST"
}
// Error: Property 'GUEST' is missing in type...const RoleLabels: Record<UserRole, string> = {
  [UserRole.Admin]: "Administrator",
  [UserRole.User]: "Regular User",
  // If you forget Guest, TS flags it here!
};

For more details on how to use these together, check out the [TypeScript Enum Handbook](https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/enums.html).
Would you like to see how to use Partial with this pattern if you don't want to define every key?

[10] [https://www.youtube.com](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WFwj3zGvU3Q)

# 4

why enum. why not union

---

Choosing between an Enum and a Union ('Admin' | 'User') usually comes down to whether you want a data structure or just a type.
When to use Enums

* Values at Runtime: Enums exist as actual objects in your JavaScript code. This makes them great for iterating (e.g., Object.values(UserRole)) to build dropdown menus or lists.
* Mapping/Refactoring: If you change Admin = "ADMIN" to Admin = "SUPER_ADMIN" in the enum definition, it updates everywhere in your app automatically.
* Metadata: You can attach JSDoc comments to enum members, which show up in your IDE's autocomplete.

When to use Unions (The Modern TS Preference)

* Lightweight: Unions disappear completely after compilation. There is zero overhead in your final JavaScript bundle.
* Better Compatibility: Unions work seamlessly with external JSON or API data. You don't have to "cast" a string from an API to an enum; if the string matches the union, it just works.
* Readability: type Status = 'open' | 'closed' is often easier to read and write than defining a separate enum block.

Comparison Summary

| Feature | Enum | Union Type |
|---|---|---|
| Runtime Object | Yes | No (Type only) |
| Iteratable | Yes | No (Requires extra array) |
| API Friendly | Needs manual mapping | Works out of the box |
| Bundle Size | Adds a small amount of code | Zero impact |

The "Industry Standard" Advice: Use Unions by default for simple sets of strings. Reach for Enums only if you need to loop through the values at runtime or want a centralized place to manage values that might change in the future.
Would you like to see how to iterate over a Union type using a "Const Array" to get the best of both worlds?

# 5

example

---

To get the benefits of a Union (lightweight) with the benefits of an Enum (iteratable), you use a const assertion.
This is the modern TypeScript way to handle a list of options.
The "Const Array" Pattern

// 1. Create a constant array (must use 'as const')const COLORS = ["red", "green", "blue"] as const;
// 2. Derive the Union type automatically from the array// This creates: type Color = "red" | "green" | "blue"type Color = typeof COLORS[number];
// 3. Use it in a Record for exhaustivenessconst ColorHex: Record<Color, string> = {
  red: "#FF0000",
  green: "#00FF00",
  blue: "#0000FF"
};
// 4. Since it's a real array, you can loop over it for UI (like a dropdown)
COLORS.forEach(color => {
  console.log(`The hex for ${color} is ${ColorHex[color]}`);
});

Why this beats an Enum:

   1. Zero Mapping: If you get "red" from an API, it automatically matches the Color type. You don't have to do ColorEnum["red"].
   2. Single Source of Truth: You define the values once in an array. The type and the runtime list are perfectly synced.
   3. No Boilerplate: You don't have to write a separate enum block and then a separate type.

Do you want to see how to use the Extract utility type to filter specific values out of this Color union?

# 6

it's weird. const as const. typeof array number.

---

It definitely looks like "code soup" at first! Here is the breakdown of why those two weird pieces are necessary:
1. The as const (The "Lock")
By default, TypeScript sees ["red", "green"] and thinks: "This is an array of any strings."
When you add as const, you are telling TS: "This array will never change, and these specific strings are the only values allowed."

* Without as const: type = string[]
* With as const: type = readonly ["red", "green"]

2. The typeof COLORS[number] (The "Extractor")
This is a trick to turn an Array (runtime) into a Union Type (compile-time).

* typeof COLORS: Gets the type of the whole array.
* [number]: This tells TypeScript: "Look at the type of any element at a numeric index inside that array."

Since the array is "locked" with as const, the only things at those indexes are "red", "green", and "blue". So, the resulting type is "red" | "green" | "blue".
The "Why"
It feels weird because you are bridging two worlds:

   1. The Runtime World: You need the COLORS array so you can use .map() or .forEach() in your actual app (like building a dropdown menu).
   2. The Type World: You need the Color union so your Record can ensure you didn't miss a color.

Think of it as: Creating a single source of truth that works for both your logic and your type safety.
Would you like to see a comparison of how this looks versus a standard interface?
